rationale: The citing paper’s core model is about gender-neutral marketing reducing reliance on product aesthetics via binary gender stereotype endorsement, whereas the cited paper studies weather → affect → hedonic consumption with gender as a moderator; the substantive constructs and mechanisms do not align beyond a broad “gender” theme. The match evidence is only a single DOI reference entry (no citation context/snippet), which is consistent with a likely background/example mention rather than direct methodological or theoretical dependence.
